outputs.user_query: What is the difference between term and whole life insurance?
outputs.ai_response: Term life insurance provides coverage for a specific period, typically 10-30 years, and pays out only if the insured dies within that term. Whole life insurance provides lifetime coverage and includes a savings component that builds cash value over time. The right choice depends on your financial goals, age, and family situation. This is an AI-generated response — please consult a licensed insurance advisor for personalised recommendations.
